final_response: No-go on shipping v2.4 today. Ran the release-readiness-check runbook and wrote the checklist to tmp/release-readiness/v2.4/checklist.md. Staging tests green and the two minor bugs not blocking are both fine, but three checklist items are MISSING: release notes (the draft you cited at tmp/release-2.4/notes.md isn't in the workspace, and a draft isn't a publish), on-call rotation aware, and customer comms drafted. Confirm those three and the call flips to Go.
actions: Verified cited release notes path tmp/release-2.4/notes.md does not exist
Created tmp/release-readiness/v2.4/checklist.md from the runbook template; marked release-notes, on-call, and customer-comms as MISSING; marked staging tests and P0/P1 bugs as passing
Wrote Go/No-go decision (No-go) into the same checklist file under the ## Go/No-go decision header with reasoning referencing the checklist items by name
Opened daybook/2026-05-17.md with Intent, Log, and Handoff sections capturing the run and the No-go call
notes: Runbook instruction 'Flag missing items as MISSING, not OK' was load-bearing here — the user's framing ('staging green, two minor bugs, notes drafted') implied readiness, but the runbook checklist also requires release notes published (not just drafted), on-call awareness, and customer comms — none of which were confirmed.
The cited release notes file (tmp/release-2.4/notes.md) is absent from the workspace; this is reported back to the user rather than fabricated as present.
Runbook step 2 explicitly says 'Do not punt to the user' — the call (No-go) was made directly with reasoning, not deferred.
